page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-24 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 90 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 3.75 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, requiring external network-attached storage for persistent data.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in specific workloads. The server achieves a strong rating in Geekbench single-core performance and image processing, alongside a strong static web serving throughput of 41.15 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is solid, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 13690.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 34968.15. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for integer and bfloat16 operations, which accelerates low-latency tasks such as text generation on small language models, where it reaches a top-tier speed of 396.71 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-standard-24 offers a balanced hardware profile for compute-intensive and general-purpose workloads without the premium cost of dedicated GPU hardware. The high L3 cache of 260 MB and memory read bandwidth of up to 1681.44 GB/sec for cached blocks support efficient data processing. This configuration is well-suited for web serving, software compilation, text processing, and moderate database operations.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-24 is an x86_64 general-purpose server instance configured with 24 dedicated vCPUs and 90 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz across 12 physical cores, it features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show strong single-core performance, high static web serving throughput of 41.15 GB/sec, and top-tier text generation speeds of 396.71 tokens/sec for small LLMs. With a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio of 3.75 GB per core, this instance is optimized for workloads such as web serving, software compilation, text processing, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-24 is a 24-vCPU, 90 GB RAM server powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It delivers strong single-core performance and high web serving throughput.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-24 features 24 vCPUs, 90 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, optimized for web serving and text processing.
tagline: A balanced 24-vCPU compute instance featuring Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for efficient web serving and text processing.
bullet_points: Powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 24 dedicated vCPUs and 90 GB RAM.
Features Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix and vector operations.
Delivers strong static web serving throughput of 41.15 GB/sec and high single-core performance.
Achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 396.71 tokens/sec for small LLM models.
Configured without local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage.
Best suited for web serving, software compilation, and text processing workloads.
